role_title: Product Manager, Banking as a Service

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in a product role
Significant time with senior customer stakeholders discussing strategy and roadmap
Owns roadmap and go to market for BaaS product area

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Brings 8+ years of experience in a product role
Strong focus on user-experiences, from front-end UI through developer docs and technical integrations
Has an obsession over product quality
Has domain knowledge in financial products, particularly card issuing, funds storage, or remittance
Has experience building platform and/or API products
Has strong written and verbal communication skills with a talent for precise articulations of customer problems
Can put themselves in the shoes of their users and be a steward of crafting great experiences
Enjoys being a generalist working on whatever it takes to solve problems and delight users

scope.surface: Banking as a Service — card programs, financial features, programmatic APIs, embedded and hosted experiences, partner integrations
core_responsibilities: Own key user journeys, from integration to core business operation
Help shape the direction, define product strategy, and establish practices to address user needs
Collaboratively partner with other Stripe teams (engineering, design, legal, risk, compliance, etc.) early in the product development lifecycle
Own the roadmap and go to market, with product marketing, of your BaaS product area
Act as the voice of the user in product and API designs, gathering direct feedback from customers
Ensure we're building in a scalable, performant way

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (BaaS, card issuing, funds storage, remittance)
Stripe is a well-known, high-credibility financial infrastructure company with strong engineering culture
Described as one of Stripe's fastest growing businesses — strong career upside signal
Explicitly calls out senior customer stakeholder engagement and strategy influence
Diverse user base across fintechs, enterprises, BNPL, SaaS, marketplaces — broad exposure
red_flags: No location, compensation, or visa sponsorship information provided in the JD
8+ years requirement with domain knowledge in card issuing/funds storage/remittance is a narrow and hard-to-bridge combination
No explicit mention of remote eligibility or office requirements — candidates cannot assess location fit

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and senior stakeholder scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and API-first platform context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location information provided in JD
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no compensation information provided
ambiguities: No location or remote policy stated anywhere in the JD — it is unclear whether this role is remote, hybrid, or onsite, and in which city/country.

The requirement for 'domain knowledge in financial products, particularly card issuing, funds storage, or remittance' uses 'or', suggesting any one area may suffice — treated as a single hard requirement with alternatives.
All bullets in 'Who you are' are stated without a 'preferred' qualifier, so all were classified as must_haves with severity: hard. Some (e.g., cultural_fit items) could arguably be soft.
